---
title: "Exploring Penguin Species"
format: dashboard
editor: source
theme: 
    - yeti
author: "Thiyanga S. Talagala"
---

```{r}
#| label: load-packages
#| message: false
#| warning: false
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)
library(scales) # For percentage formatting
library(RColorBrewer) # For Dark2 color palette
library(plotly)
library(GGally)
library(crosstalk)
```


```{r}
#| label: load-data
#| message: false
#| warning: false
data("penguins")
#if the data is saved in a CSVfile
# data <- read_csv("datafilename.csv")

```

```{r}
#| label: cleandata
names(penguins)[names(penguins) == "sex"] <- "gender"
penguins_complete <- penguins |>
  drop_na()
quartiles <- quantile(penguins_complete$body_mass, probs = c(0.1, 0.5, 0.8))

pf <- penguins_complete |>
  mutate(BMI_category = case_when(
    body_mass <= quartiles[1] ~ "Underweight",
    body_mass > quartiles[1] & body_mass <= quartiles[2] ~ "Normal",
    body_mass > quartiles[2] & body_mass <= quartiles[3] ~ "Overweight",
    body_mass > quartiles[3] ~ "Obese"
  ))

pf2 <- pf |> select(species, island, gender, BMI_category)
pf2$BMI_category <- factor(pf2$BMI_category, levels=c("Underweight", "Normal",
"Overweight", "Obese"))
```


# Overview

## Row {height="20%"}

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Adelie"

list(
  icon = "feather",
  color = "#d95f02",
  value = 146
)
```

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Chinstrap"

list(
  icon = "feather",
  color = "#7570b3",
  value = 5
)
```

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Gentoo"

list(
  icon = "feather",
  color = "#1b9e77",
  value = 20
)
```

## Row {height="40%"}

### Column {width="40%"}

```{r}
p1 <- pf |>
  ggplot(aes(x=species,  fill = factor(BMI_category))) + 
  geom_bar( position = 'dodge') + labs(fill = "BMI Category", x="Species", y="Counts") + ggtitle(" Composition of the Sample by Species and BMI Category")
ggplotly(p1)
```

### Column {width="40%"}


```{r}
p2 <- penguins |>
  ggplot(aes(x = species, fill = island)) +
  geom_bar(position = "dodge") +
  labs(title = "Composition of the Sample by Species and Island",
       x = "Species", y = "Count")
ggplotly(p2)
```

### Column {width="40%"}

```{r}
p3 <- penguins |>
  ggplot(aes(x = species, fill = gender)) +
  geom_bar(position = "dodge") +
  labs(title = "Composition of the Sample by Species and Gender",
       x = "Species", y = "Count")
ggplotly(p3)
```

# Body Size Relationships



```{r}
#| message: false
#| warning: false

pairs <- penguins |>
  select(species, bill_len, bill_dep, flipper_len, body_mass) |>
  GGally::ggpairs(
    aes(color = species),
    columns = c("bill_len", "bill_dep", "flipper_len", "body_mass")
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("darkorange", "purple", "cyan4")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("darkorange", "purple", "cyan4"))
ggplotly(pairs)
```

# Data

```{r}
#| message: false
#| warning: false

library(DT)

datatable(penguins)
```

# More Plots

## Column {.tabset}

### Map

```{r}
#| title: Map of Islands
# Install packages if needed
# install.packages(c("leaflet", "palmerpenguins", "dplyr"))

library(leaflet)
library(palmerpenguins)
library(dplyr)

# Create approximate coordinates for the islands
# (Palmer Penguins dataset does not include latitude/longitude,
# so we manually assign approximate locations)

island_map <- data.frame(
  island = c("Biscoe", "Dream", "Torgersen"),
  lat = c(-65.43, -64.73, -64.77),
  lon = c(-65.50, -64.23, -64.08)
)

# Count number of penguins by island
penguin_counts <- penguins %>%
  group_by(island) %>%
  summarise(count = n())

# Merge counts with coordinates
map_data <- left_join(island_map, penguin_counts, by = "island")

# Create leaflet map
leaflet(map_data) %>%
  addTiles() %>%
  addCircleMarkers(
    lng = ~lon,
    lat = ~lat,
    radius = ~count / 10,
    popup = ~paste0(
      "<b>Island:</b> ", island, "<br>",
      "<b>Penguin Count:</b> ", count
    ),
    label = ~island
  ) %>%
  setView(
    lng = -64.5,
    lat = -65.0,
    zoom = 6
  )
```

### Sample Size

```{r}
#| title: Chart 2
#| content: valuebox
list(
  title = "Total Penguins",
  value = nrow(penguins)
)
```

# About

Author: Dr Thiyanga S. Talagala

Email: ttalagala@sjp.ac.lk
